tasks.title: Define plugin layout surface
tasks.desc: Map the Python reference into a pure JavaScript layout engine plus a thin Obsidian integration layer. Capture vertical and horizontal layout modes, mutation boundaries, and deterministic tie-break rules.

tasks.title: Port group graph ordering
tasks.desc: Port the group dependency graph traversal and ordering logic from organize-dag-groups.py into JavaScript, preserving stable ordering and clean separations between graph analysis and geometry output.

tasks.title: Port task placement sweeps
tasks.desc: Port card sizing, lane assignment, greedy coloring, and iterative sweep placement for tasks within each group. Keep the engine pure and orientation-aware.

tasks.title: Port edge attachment optimization
tasks.desc: Port the edge-side selection and routing heuristics so the JavaScript layout preserves readable edge attachment points and deterministic tie-breaks.

tasks.title: Add layout commands and ribbon actions
tasks.desc: Add Obsidian commands and ribbon buttons for vertical and horizontal layout runs, with clear notices and active-canvas validation.

tasks.title: Apply layout in place
tasks.desc: Update the active canvas in place through the plugin without temp files, preserving non-layout metadata and writing only the required node and edge geometry changes.

tasks.title: Build fixture parity checks
tasks.desc: Compare the JavaScript layout output against existing fixture canvases and regression boards so the port stays behaviorally close to the Python reference where intended.

tasks.title: Polish plugin workflow
tasks.desc: Run manual validation in Obsidian, tighten deterministic reruns, and refine UX copy and command naming before treating the plugin as ready for review.
depends_on: Add layout commands and ribbon actions
